articles.title: Odisha schools to reopen from June 18 as monsoon brings relief from heatwave - Odisha TV
articles.full_text:
With the arrival of the southwest Monsoon in Odisha and a significant drop in summer temperatures, schools across the State are set to reopen on June 18 as per the previously announced schedule.
The decision comes after weather conditions improved, providing relief from the intense heat that had affected normal academic activities in recent weeks.
Students will return to classrooms on Thursday, marking the beginning of the new academic phase after the summer break. However, a special holiday has been declared in Sambalpur district on June 20 in view of the renowned Sital Sasthi festival, one of the region’s most celebrated cultural and religious events.
In addition to educational institutions, all government offices in Sambalpur district will also remain closed on the occasion. The festival attracts large public participation every year and holds significant cultural importance in western Odisha.
The State authorities have advised schools and educational institutions to make necessary preparations for the smooth resumption of classes as students return after the vacation period.
Worth mentioning, previously, several District Collectors had taken decisions to keep the schools closed for the then rising summer, considering the severe heat and unbearable weather, which had posed significant health risks, especially to school-going children.
The move had aimed to ensure student safety as temperatures continued to soar across Odisha. School & Mass Education (S&ME) Minister Nityananda Gond had issued a significant statement regarding the declaration of summer vacations amid rising temperatures in the State.
The Minister said that the decision on school closures will be taken based on reports submitted by district Collectors.
“Districts experiencing extreme and unbearable heat will be assessed individually, and appropriate decisions regarding holidays will be made accordingly. The safety and well-being of students remain the State government’s top priority,” the S&ME Minister had emphasised.
